lesson.menu_desc: Simple Past — Regular & Irregular Verbs, Telling a Story — 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 — What Were You Doing? — Talking About the Past With Confidence
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Vanessa Giuriati | Aula 4 — Talking About the Past

listenings.text: Let me tell you about a trip I took last year. In March, I went to the mountains with two old friends. We rented a small house near a lake. On the first day, the weather was terrible. It rained all afternoon, so we just cooked, talked, and played cards. But on the second day, everything changed. The sky was clear, and we walked for hours. We saw a waterfall, and we had lunch on a big rock. I took hundreds of photos. On the last night, we made a fire and we told old stories until very late. It was simple, but it was one of the best trips of my life. I still remember it clearly.

I want to tell you about a small surprise that made my week. Last Tuesday, I had a very long and difficult day at work. Nothing went right. When I finally got home, I felt tired and a little sad. But when I opened the door, the lights were on, and my family was there. They made my favorite dinner, and there was a cake on the table. I did not expect anything. It was not my birthday. They just wanted to say thank you for a hard year. I almost cried. I realized that a small thing, at the right moment, can change everything. That night, I went to bed happy.

extra_audio.text: First, I woke up late. Then, I missed the bus. After that, a kind stranger helped me. Finally, I arrived at work on time. At the end, I felt very lucky.
